data.title: record-test-status.sh: RED marker reverse lookup compares full .test-index line against bare test path -> marker never matched
data.description:
## Expected Behavior
When a test file is directly staged, record-test-status.sh should find its RED marker from .test-index by searching for the test file path on the RIGHT side of .test-index entries and extracting the [marker] suffix.

## Actual Behavior
The reverse lookup at lines 521-528 of record-test-status.sh greps for the test path in .test-index, then applies regex to the FULL LINE (including source:test mapping). The comparison at line 526 compares the regex capture group 1 (which includes the source prefix and colon) against the bare test path. They never match.

Example:
- .test-index line: plugins/dso/skills/sprint/SKILL.md:tests/scripts/test-sprint-primary-ticket-model.sh [test_primary_ticket_in_phase1]
- Regex group 1 captures: plugins/dso/skills/sprint/SKILL.md:tests/scripts/test-sprint-primary-ticket-model.sh
- Compared against _test_self: tests/scripts/test-sprint-primary-ticket-model.sh
- Result: NO MATCH (source prefix included in group 1)

## Root Cause
plugins/dso/hooks/record-test-status.sh line 523-526: the regex ^(.*[^[:space:]])[[:space:]]+\[([^]]+)\]$ captures everything before the marker bracket as group 1, including the source:path prefix. Line 526 then compares this full capture against the bare test path.

## Suggested Fix
After the regex match, strip the source prefix from group 1 before comparing. E.g.: _idx_test=${BASH_REMATCH[1]##*:} to extract only the test path after the last colon.
